records.ety: [OE. parchemin, perchemin, F. parchemin, LL. pergamenum, L. pergamena, pergamina, fr. L. Pergamenus of or belonging to Pergamus an ancient city of Mysia in Asia Minor, where parchment was first used.]

defs: The skin of a lamb, sheep, goat, young calf, or other animal, prepared for writing on. See Vellum.
quotes: But here's a parchment with the seal of Cæsar.

defs: The envelope of the coffee grains, inside the pulp.
